description: Routing corpus for the orchestrator — does a phrase (a) get recognized as framework-maintenance work the forge owns, and (b) route to the correct owning skill? Adversarial cases are app-consumption (hand off to adia-ui-factory), excluded ops, or off-topic. Guards the orchestrator's two gates: the scope check and the subsystem classifier.

phrases.phrase: add a new button-ui primitive with @scope tokens
author an editor-shell cluster in web-modules
promote this inline page UI into a reusable web-module
harvest a chunk into the a2ui corpus
the gen-ui retrieval MRR regressed after the corpus change
add an MCP tool to the a2ui server
add a gemini provider adapter to @adia-ai/llm
fix the SSE streaming terminal in the llm client
evolve the model registry in models.ts
score the generated UI in apps/genui against the ideal spec
run a native-primitive-leak sweep across the playgrounds and catalog
visual-probe the components site for zero-area hosts
cut v0.6.22 and tag the lockstep
author the MIGRATION GUIDE for the breaking prop rename
build a settings page in my app from existing adia-ui primitives
wire the published @adia-ai/a2ui-mcp into my SPA
add @adia-ai/llm to my chat app and stream a response
phrases.expected_alternative: adia-ui-factory (consuming the client, not producing the package)

phrases.phrase: deploy the demo site to the VM and rotate the exe.dev secrets
phrases.expected_alternative: out-of-scope (excluded ops; not in this plugin)

phrases.phrase: apply the v0.6 migration guide to sweep my downstream app
phrases.expected_alternative: adia-ui-factory (consumer applies the guide the forge authors)

phrases.phrase: explain how custom elements and the shadow DOM work in general
